description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a hip roof covered in dark shingles and a single chimney. The exterior walls are clad in light tan brick with a horizontal white trim band separating the two floors. The front features a covered porch with a set of wide steps leading up to the main entry, supported by square columns and protected by an overhanging roof. Windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with vertical rectangular panes and dark frames, and the side elevation includes a tall, narrow window. The house sits on a concrete foundation with a small landing and metal railings at the entryway. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric lines.
description: A two-story tan brick house with a hip roof, covered porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. The entryway features wide steps, columns, and a protective overhang.
description_view_0: Side view showing two pairs of windows on each floor, the brick facade, and the hip roof with chimney visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the covered porch, wide entry steps, columns, and the main door beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a tall, narrow window and a side entrance with steps and railing, plus the chimney and roof details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, and the hip roof with chimney.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with vertical rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Wide steps leading to a covered porch with square columns and an overhanging roof
